creator: andrews@TECHNOLOGIST.COM
text:
On Sat, Jul 15, 2000 at 03:58:42PM -0400, howardjp@wam.umd.edu wrote:
> >Synopsis:       New Port:  pwcrypt
> PWCrypt is a simple program that generates encrypted password hashes.
> I have needed this program more than a few times in the past year so
> I thought I'd make it available for all.

Since you are the author and distributor of this program, I will tell
you this once (as I told James Housley re healthd): I WILL NOT ALLOW
VENDORS TO PUT PATCHES IN THEIR PORTS!  PUT THEM IN YOUR DISTRIBUTION!

</rage>

Sorry, but you must put this patch:

> sed 's/^X//' >pwcrypt/patches/patch-aa << 'END-of-pwcrypt/patches/patch-aa'
> X--- Makefile.orig	Sat Jul 15 15:51:37 2000
> X+++ Makefile	Sat Jul 15 15:52:20 2000
> X@@ -6,4 +6,7 @@
> X 
> X LDADD+=	-lcrypt
> X 
> X+BINDIR=	${PREFIX}/bin
> X+MANDIR= 	${PREFIX}/man/man
> X+
> X .include <bsd.prog.mk>
> END-of-pwcrypt/patches/patch-aa

in your distribution.  It is silly to bloat FreeBSD's repository
unnecessarily when you can simply apply the patch to your own tree and
generate a new distfile with a minor version bump.

When you have done this, simply give me the version number to bump on
the port Makefile and I will import it in the tree.

Thanks,

-- 
Will Andrews <andrewsw@purdue.edu> <will@FreeBSD.org>

?O M+ V-- PS+ PE++ Y+ PGP+>+++ t++ 5 X++ R+ tv+ b++>++++ DI+++ D+
